title: The Sea Cat Appears
description: On pages 1–4, this part focuses on the sea cat appears, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: The Sea Cat Appears. The crew encounters Baroque Works’ strange forces and the danger of deception grows as Alabasta becomes harder to read. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: We should be almost there by now...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Don't let it get away! We have to hunt it down no matter what!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: The hell do you think you're doing, Viv!!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: There sure are a lot of strange creatures in the sea.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: rRnt PRNT... Heh, looks like you've still got a long ways to go if you get scared at something like that.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Let Usopp tell you about that one time he faced Sea Kings in the Calm Belt.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: The biggest Sea Kings you could imagine, my lad!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Don't worry, you'll soon be able to eat as much as you want.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Really!? What kind of cat'll come out from the sea this time!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Vivi! The wind and climate seems to have stabilized.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Yes, we must have entered Alabasta's climate.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Sea Cat we saw just now is proof of that.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: And I guess a sight like "that" is another proof that we've reached Alabasta.

summary: This part highlights the sea cat appears and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
